title: sys.monitoring: concurrent use_tool_id() races on the interpreter-global tool-name registry (monitoring_use_tool_id_impl)
description: monitoring_use_tool_id_impl does an unsynchronized check-then-act on interp->monitoring_tool_names[tool_id] (read :2190, write :2194) with no lock/critical-section/stop-the-world; concurrent sys.monitoring.use_tool_id() is a data race on interpreter-global instrumentation state, and a TOCTOU that also breaks the single-owner invariant and leaks a name reference.
race_kind: unsynchronized check-then-act (TOCTOU) on interpreter-global registry array
shared_state: interp->monitoring_tool_names[PY_MONITORING_TOOL_IDS] (per-interpreter sys.monitoring tool-name table; embedded in global _PyRuntime; pycore_interp_structs.h:1039)
severity: medium (data race + broken single-owner invariant + name refleak; sibling free_tool_id Py_CLEAR opens a use-after-free/double-free window on the same slot)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66, triggers within seconds; NT=8 threads racing a freshly-freed tool id behind a barrier)
suggested_fix: synchronize all four tool-registry accessors (use/clear/free/get). Preferred: bracket the check-then-act with _PyEval_StopTheWorld(interp)/_PyEval_StartTheWorld(interp) as _PyMonitoring_ClearToolId already does at instrumentation.c:2122. Alternative: a dedicated interpreter PyMutex held across every monitoring_tool_names[] access.
notes: Vehicle reached the site indirectly via _lsprof.Profiler.enable() -> PyObject_CallMethod(...'use_tool_id'...); repro calls sys.monitoring.use_tool_id() directly. Same class as TSAN-0011 (unsynchronized interpreter-global instrumentation registry / audit hooks). All four accessors (use_tool_id :2190/:2194, clear_tool_id :2216, free_tool_id :2242/:2248 Py_CLEAR, get_tool :2270) are plain unlocked accesses and should be fixed together. The in-file precedent for synchronizing interpreter-global monitoring state (_PyEval_StopTheWorld) already exists a few lines up.

related_issues.note: Resembles the general 'sys.monitoring is not free-threading-safe' class; genuinely unsynchronized on current main. No specific upstream issue confirmed (offline).

assessment: Real, NEW, MEDIUM: unsynchronized check-then-act (TOCTOU) on interp->monitoring_tool_names[] -- two threads pass the in-use guard, both Py_NewRef(name) (leak + dup ownership); free_tool_id Py_CLEAR opens UAF/double-free. All 4 accessors (use/clear/free/get) unlocked. Same interpreter-global-instrumentation class as TSAN-0011 audit hooks. Fix: StopTheWorld (precedent _PyMonitoring_ClearToolId:2122) or PyMutex. No existing filing. Worth umbrella.
related: TSAN-0011 (interpreter-global instrumentation state, unsynchronized -- same class)
